entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br., the crop studied for nutrient uptake and yield

entities.name: MINERAL FERTILIZER MICRO-DOSING
entities.description: A technique of applying small amounts of mineral fertilizer to improve nutrient uptake and crop yields

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: The region in Africa where the study was conducted

entities.name: SADORÉ
entities.description: The location of the ICRISAT research station in Niger

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research station is located

entities.name: SAHELIAN SANDY SOIL
entities.description: The specific soil type used in the long-term application study

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENT CHANGE
entities.description: The variation in soil nutrient levels over time under different management options

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The process of absorbing nutrients from the soil by the crop

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural output produced by the crop

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENTS DYNAMICS
entities.description: The changes and movements of nutrients within the soil over time

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to layout the study

entities.name: SOIL PH-H2O
entities.description: The pH value of the soil measured in water

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN CONTENT
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil

entities.name: NPK
entities.description: A mineral fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium

entities.name: AVAILABLE P
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus in the soil that is available for plant uptake

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The weight of the grain produced per unit area

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER
entities.description: The total biomass of the plant after water removal

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth
